seo.title: Pydantic AI Guardrails for Fintech
seo.description: Compliance guide for Pydantic AI agents in fintech. Meet SOX, PCI DSS requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Fintech teams using Pydantic AI face heightened compliance risks due to the framework's structured outputs handling sensitive financial data like transaction details and credit decisions. Without robust guardrails, common failure modes such as result validator rejections or streaming validation failures can lead to unexplainable model outputs, violating SOX, PCI DSS, and SEC regulations. Vex provides essential observability and validation to ensure AI agents meet stringent Fintech standards and prevent regulatory penalties.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires accurate financial reporting and internal controls over AI-driven processes that impact financial statements.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex result validators capture and log all agent outputs with metadata, enabling audit trails for SOX-compliant financial reporting.

complianceRequirements.description: Mandates encryption and secure handling of cardholder data in transit and at rest.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex encrypts spans containing PII and enforces data masking in traces, ensuring PCI DSS compliance during agent interactions.

complianceRequirements.description: Requires data minimization, consent, and right to explanation for automated decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex provides input/output redaction and decision explainability traces for GDPR subject access requests.

complianceRequirements.description: Demands explainable AI models for investment advice and trading decisions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex captures full agent reasoning chains and tool call traces, creating verifiable audit logs for SEC examinations.

industryRisks.name: Unexplained Credit Decisions
industryRisks.description: AI agent denies credit without auditable reasoning path, violating fair lending laws.
industryRisks.example: Pydantic AI credit scorer rejects applicant; validator fails silently, no trace available for regulatory review.

industryRisks.name: Transaction Data Exposure
industryRisks.description: Streaming responses leak unencrypted card details during failed validation.
industryRisks.example: Payment validation agent streams partial PAN data before mid-generation validation crash.

industryRisks.name: AML False Negatives
industryRisks.description: Tool retry logic masks persistent transaction monitoring failures.
industryRisks.example: Suspicious transaction flagged, but retry loop generates compliant output hiding underlying error.

industryRisks.name: Missing Financial Disclaimers
industryRisks.description: Investment advice generated without mandatory risk disclosures.
industryRisks.example: Portfolio optimizer returns recommendations without SEC-required disclaimers.

implementation.title: Add Vex Result Validator to Pydantic AI Agent
implementation.description: Integrate Vex as a result_validator to capture all structured outputs with Fintech-specific redaction.
implementation.code:
import os
from vex import Vex
from pydantic_ai import Agent

os.environ['VEX_PROJECT_ID'] = 'fintech-pydantic'
os.environ['VEX_TOKEN'] = 'your-vex-token'

vex = Vex(project_id='fintech-pydantic')

@vex.result_validator
def validate_financial_output(result: dict) -> bool:
    # SOX: Ensure disclaimers present
    if 'investment_advice' in result:
        assert 'disclaimer' in result, 'Missing SEC disclaimer'
    # PCI: Mask card data
    if 'card_number' in result:
        assert len(result['card_number']) <= 4, 'Card data not masked'
    return True

credit_agent = Agent(
    'gpt-4o',
    result_validator=validate_financial_output,
    model='financial-credit-model'
)

implementation.title: Instrument Tool Calls for AML Traceability
implementation.description: Wrap transaction monitoring tools to trace nested calls and prevent retry masking.
implementation.code:
from pydantic_ai import tool
from vex import get_current_span

@tool
def check_aml(transaction: dict):
    span = get_current_span()
    span.set_tag('aml.transaction_id', transaction['id'])
    span.set_tag('aml.customer_id', transaction['customer_id'])
    
    # Simulate AML check
    risk_score = analyze_transaction(transaction)
    
    if risk_score > 0.8:
        span.add_event('high_risk_detected')
        raise ValueError('Transaction blocked - AML violation')
    
    return {'approved': True, 'risk_score': risk_score}

payment_agent = Agent(
    'gpt-4o',
    tools=[check_aml],
    result_validator=vex.result_validator
)

implementation.title: Secure Streaming with Vex Guardrails
implementation.description: Protect streaming financial responses from mid-generation validation failures.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Pydantic AI in Fintech: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: WealthTech platform FinAI uses Pydantic AI agents for automated portfolio rebalancing and credit scoring. During a market downturn, the investment agent begins recommending aggressive positions without SEC-mandated risk disclaimers. Simultaneously, the credit scoring agent experiences nested tool call failures in its dependency injection chain, silently defaulting to approve high-risk loans due to missing AML context. Regulators discover the issues during a routine examination, revealing unencrypted transaction data in streaming logs and unexplainable credit decisions affecting 15,000 customers.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: After implementing Vex result validators on all Pydantic AI agents, FinAI gained complete observability into agent reasoning paths. The Vex dashboard revealed the disclaimer omission pattern and dependency injection failures, enabling rapid fixes. AML tool traces caught 247 high-risk transactions before approval, preventing $2.3M in potential losses. During the SEC audit, FinAI exported 100% complete reasoning traces, avoiding penalties and demonstrating compliance leadership.

cta.heading: Comply with Fintech Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Install Vex today with `pip install vex-sdk` and add result validators to your Pydantic AI agents for instant compliance.
